question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set an arbitrarily long string in the X-Forwarded-For header to cause unbounded memory allocation in the log message buffer, leading to memory exhaustion that crashes 30%+ of rosetta processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user inject newline characters into the X-Real-IP header to corrupt structured log output, causing log parsers or log aggregators to crash and indirectly take down 30%+ of dependent processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user spoof the X-Real-IP header with a fake IP address to bypass IP-based rate limiting enforced upstream (e.g., Traefik inFlightReq ipStrategy), allowing them to flood the node with requests that exhaust goroutine/thread pools and shut down 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Forwarded-For to a comma-separated list of thousands of IP addresses, causing fmt.Sprintf in TracingMiddleware to construct an extremely large log message string that exhausts heap memory across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely long URL path via request.URL.RequestURI() to cause fmt.Sprintf to allocate a very large log message string per request, leading to cumulative memory pressure that crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request path that exactly matches an entry in internalPaths (e.g., /health/liveness) to suppress Info-level logging and hide a concurrent attack from monitoring systems, allowing a secondary resource exhaustion attack to go undetected until 30%+ of nodes are down?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of requests to non-internal paths, causing log.Info() to be called for every single request and flooding the log output stream (stdout), which causes I/O blocking that starves the HTTP server goroutines and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that is a near-match to internalPaths entries (e.g., /health/liveness%00) to force Info-level logging instead of Debug-level, causing log volume amplification that fills disk and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: newTracingResponseWriter()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response that causes Write() to be called with a very large data slice, which is stored entirely in tracingResponseWriter.data in memory, leading to per-request memory accumulation that exhausts heap and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Write()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes the inner handler to write a very large response body, which is fully buffered in tracingResponseWriter.data without any size cap, causing memory exhaustion that shuts down 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that each allocate a new tracingResponseWriter with an unbounded data buffer, causing cumulative heap exhaustion across all concurrent goroutines and crashing 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set both X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers simultaneously, and since X-Real-IP takes priority, use a spoofed IP to bypass per-IP rate limiting enforced by upstream proxies, enabling a sustained request flood that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an empty X-Real-IP and a crafted X-Forwarded-For containing a loopback address (127.0.0.1) to trick upstream rate limiters into treating the request as internal/trusted, bypassing throttling and flooding 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path containing special format characters (e.g., %s, %d) that are passed unsanitized into fmt.Sprintf's message string, causing a format string confusion or unexpected string expansion that corrupts log output and destabilizes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that contains Unicode control characters or null bytes, which are logged via log.Info() without sanitization, causing log parsing failures in downstream systems that monitor node health and leading to false-positive node shutdowns affecting 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TracingMiddleware calls inner.ServeHTTP() synchronously before logging, meaning a slow-loris style attack that holds connections open causes goroutine accumulation in the middleware layer, exhausting the goroutine pool and crashing 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely long HTTP method string (request.Method) that is included in the fmt.Sprintf log message without length validation, causing large per-request allocations that cumulatively exhaust memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: internalPaths] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /health/liveness or /health/readiness at a very high rate, and since these are logged at Debug level (suppressed in production), the health check handler is invoked for every request without any rate limiting in the middleware itself, causing the health check backend to be overwhelmed and report unhealthy, triggering Kubernetes to restart 30%+ of pods?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /metrics at a high rate, and since metricsPath is in internalPaths (logged at Debug level only), the attack is invisible in production logs, allowing sustained Prometheus scrape endpoint abuse that exhausts CPU on 30%+ of nodes without triggering log-based alerts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user inject log forging payloads (e.g., newlines followed by fake log entries) into the X-Forwarded-For header, which are written verbatim into the log stream via log.Info(), causing security monitoring systems to be confused and miss real attacks that eventually shut down 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests where time.Since(start) produces an unexpectedly large duration string (e.g., by holding the connection open), causing the log message to be unusually large and contributing to log storage exhaustion that crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: WriteHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated 5xx responses from the inner handler, causing the tracingResponseWriter.statusCode to be set to error codes that are logged via log.Info(), generating high-volume error log output that fills disk and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set X-Real-IP to a value that is not a valid IP address (e.g., a very long hostname string), which passes the len(ipAddress) == 0 check and is used directly in log output without validation, causing log injection or unexpected behavior in log processors that monitor 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request body size limits in TracingMiddleware to send requests with enormous bodies that are processed by inner.ServeHTTP(), causing memory exhaustion in the handler layer while TracingMiddleware holds the goroutine open, crashing 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with query strings containing thousands of parameters appended to request.URL.RequestURI(), causing the path variable in the log message to be extremely large and exhausting memory in the logging subsystem across 30%+ of nodes?
